feed.title: SAP Community - SAP BTP, ABAP environment
feed.subtitle: SAP BTP, ABAP environment blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: The Future Does Not Fit in the Containers of the Past
entry.content:
Explore the transformative impact of technology in our global society with Rishad Tobaccowala's compelling keynote at the SAP Enterprise Architects 2024 Forum. Delve into the critical shifts in demographics, power, and mindsets that shape our future. Tobaccowala challenges us to rethink leadership and innovation strategies to thrive in an ever-changing technological landscape.

Main Recommendations from AI

These recommendations aim to guide individuals and organizations in navigating the complexities of a shifting global landscape.

 

  • Embrace Change: Encourage continuous learning and adaptation to stay relevant in a rapidly changing world.

 

  • Develop a Growth Mindset: Cultivate flexibility, resilience, and openness to new ideas.

 

  • Leverage Technology Wisely: Utilize technology, especially AI, to enhance human capabilities rather than replace them.

 

  • Prioritize Human Connection: Despite technological advances, maintain focus on human-centric values and relationships.

 

  • Lead with Vision and Empathy: Leadership should combine forward-thinking with empathy and effective communication to navigate through change.

 

On Upgrading Our Mental Operating Systems:

 

  • Spend an hour every day learning: Emphasizes the importance of dedicating time to learning every day to stay ahead of changes and not fall behind.

 

  • Once in a while, build a case for the exact opposite of what you think is true: This encourages critical thinking and the ability to understand different perspectives. If you cannot argue for the opposite of your beliefs, it's challenging to be certain of their validity. This practice also helps in anticipating competitors' moves.

 

  • Recognize we have become an age of makers and builders and creators, and try to make, build, create something that's really important: Highlights the shift towards a more creative and constructive economy and the importance of contributing through creation.

 

Remember:

“To thrive in an AI age we will have to upgrade our HI ( Human  Inspiration) to complement and enhance our impact. The future is AI + HI” -  Rishad Tobaccowala

 

Watch Rishad’s Keynote Talk

Nugget of Next: 

Subscribe to Rishad’s regular Sunday Post, I enjoy reading them every Sunday. 

Read and subscribe to Rishad’s Posts

CTA:
For Enterprise Architect SAP Community Updates and More: Connect with and Follow Paul Kurchina - https://www.linkedin.com/in/paulkurchina/ 

 

Flag Photo:

* This was our first Flag Photo with the group, we had a minor adjustment to make for future photos

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP - PostgreSQL SSH Tunnel and SSH Tunneling Explained
entry.content:
HI Yogananda ,

Thanks for the detailed blog on the PostgreSQL SSL tunnel.

Our requirement is we are developing Springboot Java application through SAP BAS by enabling PostgreSQL instance as hyperscaller option

 

Some how we are unable to connect to our PostgreSQL instance through our Java App

We are unable to connect to our PostgreSQL Server instance through PgAdmin 4.

We are encountering connection timeout errors.

Can you help me whith any documentation on how to connect to PostgreSQL through Java in BAS.

Also let me know, does the ports disable causing issue, if so how do we enable it.

 

Thanks & Regards,

Mallesh

mpyreddy@gemini-us.com

+917989981075

entry.title: Re: Introducing the new pipeline concept in Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi Alex,

Nice blog, the concept is really interesting for SAP PO migrations and responsible use of JMS queues. We have also used similar patterns for SAP PO migrations, but using a DataStore, not JMS.

Is there any reason to use JMS over the DS, for these Async Patterns, other than performance and being able to do the manual retry from the queue?

It would be really interesting to elaborate a bit on that.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: #S4HANA 1709 use case series: 3b – Advanced Variant Configuration (tech view)
entry.content:
Hi,

In AVC , Can i make mass change of configuration Profile Status from Locked to Released for 500+ materials ?

entry.title: Re: SAP Enterprise Service Management
entry.content:
Hello @pvsbprasad ,

Great Blog. It is really help.

What I understand from this Blog is that an Employee can create cases for himself within a company using SAP Service cloud V2. 

In Service Cloud V1, there as an integration between Ask HR in BTP with SAP C4C v1 to create/view Ticket directly in AskHR and it can be accessed in C4C. Do we have any such integration with AskHR with SAP Service Cloud V2 in the same manner for the Case creation and Display? Please help me with related blog.

 

Regards,

Pranoy Kumar

entry.title: Don’t Get Fooled Into Climate Change Complacency
entry.content:
A cold snap in the Ticino, on the border of Switzerland and Italy, on the slopes overlooking Lake Lugarno, resulted in a specifically poor spaghetti crop. The last few weeks of March have been a worrying time for the spaghetti farmers. There is always a chance of late frost which, while not entirely ruining the crop, does have an impact on the flavor which makes it difficult to obtain top prices on the world markets.

Another reason for the failed crop lies in the increase of the Spaghetti weevil, a tiny creature that has decimated crops in the past. Of course, spaghetti cultivation in Switzerland is nowhere near the size of the Italian industry and the vast spaghetti plantations in the Po Valley, but a bumper or failed crop can still significantly impact the prices paid on the global market based on supply and demand.

Yes, it’s April 1st and this story is based on another story first shown on April Fool’s Day in 1957 on the BBC current affairs program Panorama (check it out here, it is well worth a watch).

 

It’s a stark reminder that if we ignore climate change, it will make a fool of us all. 

Here are a few alarming climate change examples from 2023 that are no joke:

  • When a Bomb cyclone hit California in early 2023, excessive flooding (after years of drought) affected imports, exports, transportation and damaged crops all over the state.
  • In July, India announced an exportation ban on non-basmati white rice due to rising prices and fear of shortages due to El Nino disruptions.
  • The Sriracha hot source shortage was “hot” on social media. This was due to poor jalapeno crops in Mexico, which relies on water from the Colorado river which was depleted.
  • Panama Canal water levels were also very low which is putting at risk the estimated 40% of consumer goods transported between Northers Asia and the east coast of the USA.
  • In October you could read about a sugar shortage due to droughts in India, and floods in Brazil! This was compounded by a poor sugar beet harvest in Europe.

 

Be part of the solution not the problem

It’s imperative that companies make sustainability central to their overall business strategy. To begin the process, business leaders must examine all functions of their network and identify sustainability weaknesses within and outside of their organization.

For example, supply chains are a major contributor to waste and carbon emissions and are therefore a major area of opportunity for improvements. Creating measurable goals to improve a company’s supply chain (e.g., eliminating single-use plastics within the next 18 months, or becoming carbon neutral by 2030,) can restore the confidence of customers, investors, employees and other stakeholders that the organization is making active strides to become more sustainable.

But setting goals is one thing and meeting them is another. Supply chain leaders must make sure they are accounting for and managing climate-related emissions across all parts of their supply chain (Scope 1,2 and 3), which includes suppliers, contract manufacturers, logistics service providers and other trading partners.

This is no small feat – a lack of visibility across a company’s supply chain is typically the reason why business leaders struggle to manage the total carbon footprint of products. By employing technology to provide the visibility they need to see across their supply chain, business leaders can then take actions to reduce their company’s end-to-end carbon footprint.

To learn more about how to get your business ready to enable a risk-resilient and sustainable supply chain, download the recent Oxford Economics Research.

Follow me on Twitter.

entry.title: Re: SAP Data Intelligence Python Operators and Cloud Connector – TCP
entry.content: Thanks for this . Is there a nodejs corresponding version of the codebase ?

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!

It is great to see everyone engaged on products and topics of interest plus local meetups are fun too! Best of luck!

entry.title: Re: Get Certified: C_HANATEC_17
entry.content:
Hello @HayBouten ,

The Certification C_HCADM_05 : Provision and administer databases in SAP HANA Cloud expired on 28th March 2024.


I didn't see any new version of the certificate in list of new certificates : https://training.sap.com/certification/validity

What is the new version of this certification? 

I see the learning journey of this still exists : Provision and Administer Databases in SAP HANA Cloud | SAP Learning

and this learning journey say leads to certification.

<![if !vml]>

 

<![endif]><![if !vml]>

 

<![endif]>

 So which certification does this leads to? because I don't want to spend hours of learning time which leads to nothing.
So, please let me know  which certification to choose after learning  Provision and Administer Databases in SAP HANA Cloud | SAP Learning

Thanks and Regards,
Revanth Kanteerava

entry.title: Re: Create Custom Field F4 Help View using CDS for In App Extension
entry.content:
Hi @SamannayaRoy ,

Thanks for this informative blog. I already added a F4 Value help in ME21N.

Now I want to filter my Value Help with Vendor code which is used in PO Creation only (when user click on F4 then only single record will come against Vendor Code).

and this is required in In-App Extensibility only because my client does not have BTP.

how to fulfill this requirement in @Sap S4 Hana Public cloud.

please suggest for the same.


Regards,

Anurag

SAP BTP, ABAP environment   SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP Environment

entry.title: Re: Announcing General Availability of SAP Build Code: Speed up development with generative AI
entry.content: @clemrngt at the time of release of SAP Build Code you are not able to use Joule to product ABAP.  Build Code does allow for you to connect to SAP systems and consume ABAP based API's.

entry.title: Re: Create Custom Field F4 Help View using CDS for In App Extension
entry.content: Good to hear you got help from the blog. You can get it filtered into the CDS view itself. Take data source I*Purchaseorder* from type: ddls.

entry.title: Group Reporting Data Audit
entry.content:
In this blog, we are going to share with you some insights with regards to the audit feature in Group Reporting from technical perspective. In BPC NetWeaver standard model, it is possible to track transaction data change as well as activities / tasks. For Group Reporting which is part of S/4 HANA, it might be possible to achieve the similar functionality as following.

1. Master Data Change Log
    The majority of the Group Reporting master data table is delivered with "log activated" which could be confirmed via SE11 -> Technical  Settings. For example, table FINCS_BUNIT is used to store consolidation unit master data.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

It is possible to track the data change log via transaction code SCU3 if system is configured properly as per note 2437986 - SCU3 | How to enable logging in the system.

2. Transaction Data Change Log

Each of group reporting tasks will log the data posting in the log table such as FINCS_LOG_HEADER (Log Header) and FINCS_LOG_ITEM (Log Details). So it is possible to track the data change by leveraging such consolidation log.
If some tasks are enabled delta posting (majority of the reclassification tasks as of S/4 HANA 2022), which means the execution will not remove the previous populated data, then ACDOCU table itself could be leveraged to track transaction data change.

3. Activity Log

Transaction code STAD records all the user activities in the system and keep such records for three days. Such statistic log could be leveraged to track user activities similar to GRC AC feature.

Hope the above information could give you some idea on how to build custom audit program to display the audit report similar to BPC.

entry.title: Generative AI-based SAP Fiori App development with Joule directly from the business requirement
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Build Code, operating within SAP Business Technology Platform, is an application development environment for Java and JavaScript, providing developers with a turnkey solution for coding, testing, integration and application life cycle management. It provides a simplified developer experience, AI-based code generation with Joule copilot that drives productivity, is tailored for SAP development and supports fusion development by teams of professional and citizen developers.   

This blog focuses on leveraging Joule’s generative AI features for SAP Fiori application development directly from the business requirement description(user story) prompt.

 

Business Requirement

Create an SAP Fiori application as per the requirements outlined in the user story below:

User Story

As a contract administrator, I want to create and manage contracts and ‘parties involved’ information in the system, so that I can effectively track and handle legal agreements and ‘parties involved’ interactions.

Contracts can cover a vast range of agreements, such as sales contracts, service agreements and employment contracts.

Party involved: A ‘party involved’ is an individual, organization, or entity that purchases goods, products, or services from another party, typically a business or seller.

Common attributes of a contract might include:

  1. Contract ID: A unique identifier for the contract.
  2. Party involved Identification Number: A unique identification number for the party involved.
  3. Contract Type: The type of contract (e.g. sales, service, employment).
  4. Start Date: The date on which the contract becomes valid.
  5. End Date: The date on which the contract expires (if applicable).
  6. Status: The current status of the contract (draft, active, expired, terminated, etc.)

A typical ‘party involved’ has the following attributes:

  1. Party involved Identification Number
  2. Party involved Name: It refers to the given name of a person or entity, or a label by which they are addressed or identified.
  3. Party involved Address: It refers to a physical location where an individual, business, or entity is situated or can be reached.
  4. Party involved Contact Information

Acceptance Criteria

Scenario 1: List All Contracts

Given I am logged into the contract management system, when I launch the SAP Fiori application to maintain contracts, then I should be able to view the list of all the contracts in a list without pressing the GO button. The list of all the contracts should have : Contract ID, Party involved Identification Number, Contract Type and Start Date. The list of filters should include Contract Type, Contract Status and Start Date.

Scenario 2: View Contract Details 

Given I am logged into the contract management system, when I select a specific contract from the list of SAP Fiori application, then I should be able to view the contract details and parties involved information.

 

Joule's Generative AI-based SAP Fiori App Development

Prerequisites

  • Get a Free Account on SAP BTP Trial
  • Setup SAP Build Code

1. Create a New Project using SAP Build Code

1.1.  Navigate to the SAP Build lobby

1.2.  Click on Create

Create a Project

 

 

 

 

 

 

1.3 Click the Build an Application tile

Build an Application

1.4  Click the SAP Build Code tile

SAP Build Code

1.5  Click the Full-Stack Application tile

Full-Stack Application

1.6 Create a full stack project.

Contract Management Project

 

2. Create an SAP Fiori App via Prompt

2.1.  Click the project to open it in SAP Business Application Studio

2.2.  Click the Open Guide

Open Guide

2.3. Expand the 'SAP Fiori UI Node'

Expand the 'Create an SAP Fiori Application Directly from Your Business Requirements' node. Click the Open Joule button to launch Joule's AI assistant for SAP Fiori tools.

Joule's SAP Fiori Tools Generative AI

2.4. Send requirement prompt to Joule

Prompt the user story description(refer to Business Requirement section above) to Joule and click send.

Send business requirement description to Joule

Joule now checks the input against the list of features it supports and implements them.

Joule's artifacts creation based on prompt

Click Show Result to review the generated artifacts. If fine, accept the changes to generate the project in your file system.

Accept Project Files

2.5. Preview the generated application

Click run and debug.

Run and Debug

The application's preview is displayed.

Application Preview

Launch the contract management app.

Contract Management

Select a contract from the list to view its details.

Select Contract

Contract details

Conclusion

Joule's SAP Fiori tools generative AI can really expedite the SAP Fiori development by directly translating the business requirements into an SAP Fiori elements application containing a list report and one or two object pages as part of a CAP project. The generated application can further be adapted to accommodate more complex functionalities.

With SAP Build Code, application is developed automatically following the "golden path," utilising SAP best practices and integrated assistance to build quickly and integrate seamlessly.

 

Further Information

SAP Build Code

 

 

Please feel free to add a comment, should you have any queries.

Best Regards,
Anuradha Dhingan

entry.title: Unleashing AI Magic: Building Full-Stack Apps with SAP Build Code
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Build Code is a turn-key environment for coding, testing, integrating, and managing applications by bringing together key design time, runtime services and tools in one single offering.
It introduces an end-to-end integrated solution where SAP technologies and AI combine, greatly enhancing the productivity of developers.
With SAP Build Code, developers can build rapidly using AI code generation with Joule copilot.
Refer to this blog - link for more details on SAP Build Code and the features it provides.

Motivation

SAP Build Code has garnered a lot of attention since its release this week and I was curious to try out what all it has to offer. So here I am, sharing with you how I leveraged SAP Build Code to create two different full stack applications with just a few clicks and prompts.

Getting Started

Pre-requisites
SAP BTP sub-account with SAP Build Code set up. Boosters are available for setting it up on BTP Trial Account.
Additionally, if you want to deploy the application, you need the Cloud Foundry runtime environment.

Creating the project

  1. Click on SAP Build Code under Instances and Subscriptions in the BTP sub-account and navigate to the lobby. Click on Create.
  2. Select Build an Application -> SAP Build Code -> Full Stack Application.
  3. Enter Project Name, Namespace and Service Name.
    Select the dev space for Full Stack Application.
  4. Click on the newly created project.

Creating the application using Business Application Studio

  1. Once inside the Business Application Studio, click on the Joule icon in the left navigation bar and click on Open Guide.
  2. You can now see a detailed approach to Generative AI Powered development.

     

Now, there are 2 ways you can go about this.

  1. Using the option SAP Fiori UI from Generative AI Powered Development, you can provide your business requirements in natural language and Joule generates the entire source code for your business app in one go. You can accept the entire code and can later make adjustments using AI / through minimal coding to get the desired outcome or, alternatively ask AI to regenerate the code for you.
    Refer to this video to watch how I created a Product Orders Management App to demonstrate this approach.

  2. You can go step by step creating the CAP data model and service first, then the sample data and app logic, add some test cases and then create the UI using templates. To demonstrate this approach, I will be creating a Library Management App.
    (See detailed instructions below)

    Data Model and Service Creation -

    Click on Open Joule.
    Describe the CAP models/entities that you want to create as an input to Joule. For example -

    "Create an app for library management system for students. It should have entities - books, authors, students. Books should have issueDate, publicationDate and genre. Students must have studentId, phone and email. Books are issued to students and have author."

    Check the output created by Joule and if satisfied click accept.
    You can edit the prompt and ask Joule to regenerate again.
    You can also copy the code snippet and paste it in the schema.cds file.
    Check the sample data created in the db folder and also the service.cds file for the service entities created. 

    Sample Data Generation - 

    Although sample data is created by default, you can ask Joule to create sample data for you based on your needs.
    Click on Open Joule under Sample Data - > Generate Sample Data.

     

    Describe the sample data you want from Joule.
    You again have the options to either accept the sample data generated or ask Joule to regenerate.
    At any point of the app creation, you also have the option to enhance the sample data.
    Go to Sample Data Editor and click on Enhance.
    This will again open the Joule Assistant.
    Enter the enhancement you need.
    For example - 
    Make sure email ids of students are @smu.com.


    Application Logic - 

    We will be creating logic to tag new books published in the current year with “-- NEW ARRIVAL”.
    The logic will be triggered every time the books are fetched/read.

    Click on Create Application Logic under Application Logic.

    Click on Open CDS Graphical Modeler.
    To the books service, click on Add Logic.
    Select Service entity Books.

    No action / function.

    Name the App Logic – tag-newbooks.

    Click on Add.

    Select, the Phase – “After”


    Select the Standard Event – Read.

    Next, click on Open Code Editor, and click on Application Logic.
    We will ask Joule to now add application logic.

    Use input – "Create logic to add "-- NEW ARRIVAL” to the name of books." which are published in current year."

    Accept if you are satisfied with the result and app logic files will be created for you under the folder srv/code.

     

    Run your application using cds watch and you will be able to preview your application in the browser.


    Unit tests - 

    Let's be honest, writing unit tests is not the most liked part of development.
    SAP Build Code is a saviour when it comes to that.
    (Yes, you heard that right, it can create unit tests for you.😍)

    Go to “Create Unit Test” under Generative AI Powered Development and click on Open CDS Graphical Modeler.
    Select the app logic created earlier for books service and click on Unit test.

     

    It takes us to the Joule assistant where I will continue with the default input provided.

    "Generate a unit test case for my application logic".

     

    Once you accept the test case generated, it updates the code in the test file.

     

    Run the test and it should be successful.

    If not, update the tests as needed.

    UI Application - 

    SAP Build Code provides predefined templates for UI and a wizard based approach.

    Under UI applications in the story board, click on +.
    Specify a name, display name for your application and click on Next.
    You can create template based web applications that can launched in the browser or create native iOS / android apps.

    Select the first one and click on Next.
    Select from a list of templates, I am choosing the first one which is List Report Page and click Next.
    Select the Main Entity and fill in other details as needed and click on Finish.
     
    The UI app is now created and linked to the service.
     
    Run the application again and in the browser you can additionally now see a web app link.

    Click on that, and you are redirected to the list report page with the list of books displayed.

     

     

Voila! With that we have created our full stack application that we can now deploy and run in production.
How cool! Isn't it!
Build Code can also be used for Fusion Development where business experts/citizen developers and professional developers can collaborate to create business applications.


Have you tried out SAP Build Code yet?
Share your thoughts in the comments section.

entry.title: 入門！SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning 機能紹介シリーズまとめ
entry.content:
本ブログシリーズはSAP Analytics CloudのPlanning機能をこれから学ぶ方を対象とし、予算計画などの一連の予算管理業務に必要なSAP Analytics Cloudのそれぞれの機能の特徴や要点をご紹介します。

ブログは随時追加予定です。（最終更新 ‐ 2024/4/1）
是非、このページをブックマークして、定期的にチェックしていただけると嬉しいです。

※機能の分類については一例です。

※SAP Analytics Cloudの基本操作やPlanning機能紹介シリーズ以外のSAP Analytics Cloudに関するBlogをチェックしたい場合には、併せてSAP Analytics Cloud関連ブログまとめもご覧ください。

SAP Analytics Cloud for planning基礎

  • SAP Analytics Cloud for planning概要：SAP Analytics Cloud for planningの全体像および特徴（2024/4/1）
  • モデルの設計：データの格納先であるキューブの設計（今後追加予定）

計画入力/シミュレーション

  • マニュアル入力：データの表示と計画値のハンド入力（今後追加予定）
  • データロック：計画作業で変更できないデータ領域の設定（今後追加予定）
  • データチェック（バリデーションチェック/誘導）：入力可能な項目の組み合わせのチェックと誘導（今後追加予定）
  • バリュードライバーツリー：KPIツリーを利用したドライバーベースの計画およびシミュレーション（今後追加予定）
  • 予測：過去データに基づく予測計画およびシミュレーション（今後追加予定）

データ加工/処理

  • データアクションとマルチアクション：割当や通貨換算などの計算処理ステップの順次処理機能（今後追加予定）
  • 割当：配賦の設定（今後追加予定）
  • 通貨換算：通貨換算の設定（今後追加予定）
  • 高度な式：スクリプトを利用した柔軟な計算処理の設定（今後追加予定）

計画タスク管理（ワークフロー）

  • コメント入力：ユーザ間のコラボレーションのためのコメント機能（今後追加予定）
  • カレンダー：タスクおよびワークフロー管理（今後追加予定）

システム管理

  • ユーザ管理/権限管理：アプリケーション機能、コンテンツ利用、データアクセス等に関するセキュリティ設定（今後追加予定）
  • 監査：トランザクションデータの変更履歴の記録（今後追加予定）

拡張

  • スクリプト：JavaScriptによるレポートおよび計画入力画面の拡張（今後追加予定）

 

※機能の詳細ついてはSAP Help Portalをご参照ください。

※ SAP Analytics Cloudは、四半期に１度のペースで製品のバージョンアップが実施されます。そのため今後のバージョンアップにより、本ブログで紹介する画面キャプチャや、操作が異なる可能性があることをご了承ください。

entry.title: 入門！SAP Analytics Cloud for planning 機能紹介シリーズ SAP Analytics Cloud for planning 概要
entry.content:
本ブログシリーズはSAP Analytics CloudのPlanning機能をこれから学ぶ方を対象とし、予算計画などの一連の予算管理業務に必要なSAP Analytics Cloudのそれぞれの機能の特徴や要点をご紹介します。

※機能の詳細ついてはSAP Help Portalを参照ください。

※ SAP Analytics Cloudは、四半期に１度のペースで製品のバージョンアップが実施されます。そのため今後のバージョンアップにより、本ブログで紹介する画面キャプチャや、操作が異なる可能性があることをご了承ください。（本ブログは2024年1月に作成しています。）

SAPのData ＆ Analyticsソリューションの全体像

SAP Analytics Cloudはデータの分析・可視化を行うBI機能、予算の計画・管理を行うPlanning機能、機械学習を活用したPredictive機能を一つのプラットフォームとして提供しています。例えばplanning機能を利用して立てた予算と実績のデータを合わせてBI機能で予実差異の分析を行うダッシュボードを作成する、分析にあたってはPredictive機能を利用して予実差異の原因を自動提案させる、そして分析結果をもとに今後の見込を作成するという形で、それぞれの機能をシームレスに組み合わせてお使いいただけます。
SAPでは企業全体で整合性のとれた業務プロセスを実現するERPとしてSAP S/4HANA、そしてS/4HANAやその他のSAPシステム、SAP以外のシステムからデータを集約するデータウェアハウスとしてSAP Datasphereを提供しており、SAP Analytics CloudはS/4HANAとDatasphereの双方に対してアクセスし、データを活用することが可能です。

本ブログシリーズでは予算管理を行うSAP Analytics Cloudのplanning機能についてご紹介します。

 

SAP Analytics Cloud for planningのソリューション全体像

SAP Analytics Cloud for planningでは予算管理に必要な機能を網羅しており、予算管理業務をクラウドで一元化することで業務を効率化します。

SAP Analytics Cloud for planningでは予算のもとになる実績データの情報収集から、トップダウンのガイドライン提示、ガイドラインに従ったボトムアップでの予算の入力、入力後のデータの集計や按分処理、予算のバージョン比較やシミュレーションを行い予算を最終化、そして立てた予算をもとに業務を行った結果として業務システムから収集した実績データと合わせて予実管理や今後の予算修正という一連の業務プロセスをカバーしています。

 

各業務プロセスやタスクに必要な機能も網羅しています。
各機能の概要と要点については以下のまとめページ（入門！SAP Analytics Cloud for planning機能紹介シリーズまとめ）から各ブログをご確認ください。

 

SAP Analytics Cloud for Planningによる予算管理の流れ・全体像

SAP Analytics Cloudのコンテンツはデータを格納する箱である「モデル」とレポートやダッシュボード、予算入力画面を作成する「ストーリー」から構成されます。
モデルはディメンション（分析軸）やメジャー（金額や数量などの評価項目）で構成され組織階層や会計年度、権限などを管理します。
そのモデルに対して、ストーリーで作成した予算入力画面またはExcelインターフェース（SAP Analytics Cloud add-in for MS Office）から予算の入力を行います。また計画策定に必要なデータをS/4HANA等の他システムやファイルからインポートすることも可能です。
そしてモデルに蓄積されたデータをストーリーで自由に画面を設計して予算集計や業績管理、予実差異分析、シミュレーションに使っていただくことができます。

 

SAP Analytics Cloudの特長を活かした使い方

SAP Analytics CloudはIT部門や導入ベンダーによる構築だけではなく、業務ユーザがセルフサービスで直接作成、運用していただくセルフサービスBI・Planningとしてご利用いただくこともできます。
他システムとのデータの接続やモデルの作成などのデータの準備はIT部門や導入ベンダーが事前に行い、それを利用して業務ユーザ自身で業務に必要な分析や計画入力のための画面を作成、利用していただくことも可能です。

 
またSAP Analytics CloudではSAPデータソースとの連携を含む事前定義済みのレポートテンプレートとして「ビジネスコンテンツ」をご提供しています。

S/4HANAとSAP Analytics Cloudを利用した統合財務計画(Integrated Financial Planning for SAP S/4HANA)のテンプレートではS/4HANAのマスタデータや実績データを利用した計画を行うことができ、営業費用計画や販売計画等を順次行い、財務諸表計画に統合するプロセスをご用意しています。
一からコンテンツを作成していただくことも可能ですが、ビジネスコンテンツを利用していただくことでテンプレートを利用した構築やテンプレートをもとに各社様の要件に合わせて一部修正して使うなど効率的な導入を行っていただくことも可能です。

Integrated Financial Planningの詳細はこちらをご覧ください。

 

最後に：SAP Analytics Cloud for planning のポイント

ここまでご紹介したSAP Analytics Cloud for planningのポイントをまとめると以下です。

  • BI/Planning/Predictive 三位一体
    予算管理業務に必要な機能を網羅している上、一つの製品で分析と予算管理及び機械学習の利用が同時に可能
  • SAP製品との親和性とテンプレート
    接続性はもちろん、各種SAPアプリケーションに応じたビジネスコンテンツ（テンプレート）も提供
  • 既製服アプローチ、オーダーメイドアプローチ、セミオーダーアプローチ
    ➀ ビジネスコンテンツを利用した既製服アプローチ、② 要件ごとにカスタマイズされたオーダーメイドアプローチ、③ ➀②をハイブリッドにしたセミオーダーアプローチ、が可能。
  • セルフサービスBI/PlanningとITアプローチのハイブリッド
    業務部門主導での開発/運用が可能だが、IT部門管轄での作り込んだアプリケーション開発/運用も可能

 

今回はSAP Analytics Cloud for planning概要のご紹介でした。SAP Analytics Cloudは予算計画などの一連の予算管理業務に必要な機能を備えており、本ブログシリーズではそれぞれの機能の特徴や要点をご紹介しています。

こちらのリンクからご参照ください。

entry.title: Re: Bar Code Scanning in SAP ByD
entry.content:
Very well described process Sankaran!

There is also a status column in the bar code input called Approved.....If we manually enter the barcode it shows as not approved. can you pls let me know how to do the scanning for each product ID? I tried your process but system doesn;t accept manually entered bar codes shows as not approved.

Currently we are using the bar code only per delivery but researching more for detailed scanning.
